verses.latin_vulgate: Fuit vir unus de Ramathaimsophim, de monte Ephraim, et nomen ejus Elcana, filius Jeroham, filii Eliu, filii Thohu, filii Suph, Ephrathæus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִי֩ אִ֨ישׁ אֶחָ֜ד מִן־הָרָמָתַ֛יִם צוֹפִ֖ים מֵהַ֣ר אֶפְרָ֑יִם וּשְׁמ֡וֹ אֶ֠לְקָנָ֠ה בֶּן־יְרֹחָ֧ם בֶּן־אֱלִיה֛וּא בֶּן־תֹּ֥חוּ בֶן־צ֖וּף אֶפְרָתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: There was a man of Ramathaimsophim, of Mount Ephraim, and his name was Elcana, the son of Jeroham, the son of Eliu, the son of Thohu, the son of Suph, an Ephraimite:
verses.finnish_biblia: Yksi mies oli Ramataimin Zophimista Ephraimin vuorelta, ja hänen nimensä oli Elkana Jerohamin poika, Elihun pojan, Tohun pojan, Zuphin pojan, joka oli Ephratista.
verses.king_james: Now there was a certain man of Ramathaim–zophim, of mount Ephraim, and his name was Elkanah, the son of Jeroham, the son of Elihu, the son of Tohu, the son of Zuph, an Ephrathite:

verses.latin_vulgate: et habuit duas uxores, nomen uni Anna, et nomen secundæ Phenenna. Fueruntque Phenennæ filii : Annæ autem non erant liberi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלוֹ֙ שְׁתֵּ֣י נָשִׁ֔ים שֵׁ֤ם אַחַת֙ חַנָּ֔ה וְשֵׁ֥ם הַשֵּׁנִ֖ית פְּנִנָּ֑ה וַיְהִ֤י לִפְנִנָּה֙ יְלָדִ֔ים וּלְחַנָּ֖ה אֵ֥ין יְלָדִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he had two wives, the name of one was Anna, and the name of the other Phenenna. Phenenna had children: but Anna had no children.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hänellä oli kaksi vaimoa, yhden nimi oli Hanna ja toisen nimi Peninna; ja Peninnalla oli lapsia, mutta Hannalla ei ollut lasta.
verses.king_james: And he had two wives; the name of the one was Hannah, and the name of the other Peninnah: and Peninnah had children, but Hannah had no children.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ascendebat vir ille de civitate sua statutis diebus, ut adoraret et sacrificaret Domino exercituum in Silo. Erant autem ibi duo filii Heli, Ophni et Phinees, sacerdotes Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעָלָה֩ הָאִ֨ישׁ הַה֤וּא מֵֽעִירוֹ֙ מִיָּמִ֣ים ׀ יָמִ֔ימָה לְהִֽשְׁתַּחֲוֺ֧ת וְלִזְבֹּ֛חַ לַיהֹוָ֥ה צְבָא֖וֹת בְּשִׁלֹ֑ה וְשָׁ֞ם שְׁנֵ֣י בְנֵֽי־עֵלִ֗י חׇפְנִי֙ וּפִ֣נְחָ֔ס כֹּהֲנִ֖ים לַיהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And this man went up out of his city upon the appointed days, to adore and to offer sacrifice to the Lord of hosts in Silo. And the two sons of Heli, Ophni and Phinees, were there priests of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Se mies meni vuosivuodelta kaupungistansa Siloon rukoilemaan ja uhraamaan Herralle Zebaotille; ja siellä oli kaksi Elin poikaa, Hophni ja Pinehas, Herran papit.
verses.king_james: And this man went up out of his city yearly to worship and to sacrifice unto the Lord of hosts in Shiloh. And the two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, the priests of the Lord, were there.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit ergo dies, et immolavit Elcana, deditque Phenennæ uxori suæ, et cunctis filiis ejus et filiabus, partes :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י הַיּ֔וֹם וַיִּזְבַּ֖ח אֶלְקָנָ֑ה וְנָתַ֞ן לִפְנִנָּ֣ה אִשְׁתּ֗וֹ וּֽלְכׇל־בָּנֶ֛יהָ וּבְנוֹתֶ֖יהָ מָנֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now the day came, and Elcana offered sacrifice, and gave to Phenenna, his wife, and to all her sons and daughters, portions:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sinä päivänä, jona Elkana uhrasi, antoi hän emännällensä Peninnalle ja kaikille hänen pojillensa ja tyttärillensä osat.
verses.king_james: And when the time was that Elkanah offered, he gave to Peninnah his wife, and to all her sons and her daughters, portions:

verses.latin_vulgate: Annæ autem dedit partem unam tristis, quia Annam diligebat. Dominus autem concluserat vulvam ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּלְחַנָּ֕ה יִתֵּ֛ן מָנָ֥ה אַחַ֖ת אַפָּ֑יִם כִּ֤י אֶת־חַנָּה֙ אָהֵ֔ב וַיהֹוָ֖ה סָגַ֥ר רַחְמָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But to Anna he gave one portion with sorrow, because he loved Anna. And the Lord had shut up her womb.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Hannalle antoi hän yhden osan murehtien, sillä hän rakasti Hannaa; mutta Herra oli sulkenut hänen kohtunsa.
verses.king_james: But unto Hannah he gave a worthy portion; for he loved Hannah: but the Lord had shut up her womb.

verses.latin_vulgate: Affligebat quoque eam æmula ejus, et vehementer angebat, in tantum ut exprobraret quod Dominus conclusisset vulvam ejus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְכִעֲסַ֤תָּה צָרָתָהּ֙ גַּם־כַּ֔עַס בַּעֲב֖וּר הַרְּעִמָ֑הּ כִּֽי־סָגַ֥ר יְהֹוָ֖ה בְּעַ֥ד רַחְמָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Her rival also afflicted her, and troubled her exceedingly, insomuch that she upbraided her, that the Lord had shut up her womb:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hänen vainoojansa vaivasi häntä ja soimasi hänelle, että Herra oli sulkenut hänen kohtunsa.
verses.king_james: And her adversary also provoked her sore, for to make her fret, because the Lord had shut up her womb.

verses.latin_vulgate: sicque faciebat per singulos annos : cum redeunte tempore ascenderent ad templum Domini, et sic provocabat eam : porro illa flebat, et non capiebat cibum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְכֵ֨ן יַעֲשֶׂ֜ה שָׁנָ֣ה בְשָׁנָ֗ה מִדֵּ֤י עֲלֹתָהּ֙ בְּבֵ֣ית יְהֹוָ֔ה כֵּ֖ן תַּכְעִסֶ֑נָּה וַתִּבְכֶּ֖ה וְלֹ֥א תֹאכַֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And thus she did every year, when the time returned, that they went up to the temple of the Lord: and thus she provoked her: but Anna wept, and did not eat.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja niin hän teki joka vuosi, kuin he menivät Herran huoneesen, ja saatti hänen murheelliseksi. Mutta hän itki ja ei syönyt.
verses.king_james: And as he did so year by year, when she went up to the house of the Lord, so she provoked her; therefore she wept, and did not eat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit ergo ei Elcana vir suus : Anna, cur fles ? et quare non comedis ? et quam ob rem affligitur cor tuum ? numquid non ego melior tibi sum, quam decem filii ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר לָ֜הּ אֶלְקָנָ֣ה אִישָׁ֗הּ חַנָּה֙ לָ֣מֶה תִבְכִּ֗י וְלָ֙מֶה֙ לֹ֣א תֹֽאכְלִ֔י וְלָ֖מֶה יֵרַ֣ע לְבָבֵ֑ךְ הֲל֤וֹא אָֽנֹכִי֙ ט֣וֹב לָ֔ךְ מֵעֲשָׂרָ֖ה בָּנִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Elcana, her husband, said to her: Anna, why weepest thou? and why dost thou not eat? and why dost thou afflict thy heart? Am not I better to thee than ten children?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Elkana hänen miehensä sanoi hänelle: Hanna, mitäs itket? ja miksi et syö? ja minkätähden sinun sydämes on murheellinen? enkö minä sinulle parempi ole kuin kymmenen poikaa?
verses.king_james: Then said Elkanah her husband to her, Hannah, why weepest thou? and why eatest thou not? and why is thy heart grieved? am not I better to thee than ten sons?

verses.latin_vulgate: Surrexit autem Anna postquam comederat et biberat in Silo. Et Heli sacerdote sedente super sellam ante postes templi Domini,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתָּ֣קׇם חַנָּ֔ה אַחֲרֵ֛י אׇכְלָ֥הֿ בְשִׁלֹ֖ה וְאַחֲרֵ֣י שָׁתֹ֑ה וְעֵלִ֣י הַכֹּהֵ֗ן יֹשֵׁב֙ עַל־הַכִּסֵּ֔א עַל־מְזוּזַ֖ת הֵיכַ֥ל יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: So Anna arose after she had eaten and drunk in Silo: And Heli, the priest, sitting upon a stool before the door of the temple of the Lord;
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin nousi Hanna, sittekuin hän oli syönyt ja juonut Silossa; ja pappi Eli istui istuimella Herran templin pihtipielen tykönä,
verses.king_james: So Hannah rose up after they had eaten in Shiloh, and after they had drunk. Now Eli the priest sat upon a seat by a post of the temple of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: cum esset Anna amaro animo, oravit ad Dominum, flens largiter,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהִ֖יא מָ֣רַת נָ֑פֶשׁ וַתִּתְפַּלֵּ֥ל עַל־יְהֹוָ֖ה וּבָכֹ֥ה תִבְכֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: As Anna had her heart full of grief, she prayed to the Lord, shedding many tears,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Hanna oli sangen katkeralla sydämellä, ja rukoili Herraa, ja itki hartaasti,
verses.king_james: And she was in bitterness of soul, and prayed unto the Lord, and wept sore.

verses.latin_vulgate: et votum vovit, dicens : Domine exercituum, si respiciens videris afflictionem famulæ tuæ, et recordatus mei fueris, nec oblitus ancillæ tuæ, dederisque servæ tuæ sexum virilem : dabo eum Domino omnibus diebus vitæ ejus, et novacula non ascendet super caput ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּדֹּ֨ר נֶ֜דֶר וַתֹּאמַ֗ר יְהֹוָ֨ה צְבָא֜וֹת אִם־רָאֹ֥ה תִרְאֶ֣ה ׀ בׇּעֳנִ֣י אֲמָתֶ֗ךָ וּזְכַרְתַּ֙נִי֙ וְלֹא־תִשְׁכַּ֣ח אֶת־אֲמָתֶ֔ךָ וְנָתַתָּ֥ה לַאֲמָתְךָ֖ זֶ֣רַע אֲנָשִׁ֑ים וּנְתַתִּ֤יו לַֽיהֹוָה֙ כׇּל־יְמֵ֣י חַיָּ֔יו וּמוֹרָ֖ה לֹא־יַעֲלֶ֥ה עַל־רֹאשֽׁוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she made a vow, saying: O Lord of hosts, if thou wilt look down, and wilt be mindful of me, and not forget thy handmaid, and wilt give to thy servant a manchild: I will give him to the Lord all the days of his life, and no razor shall come upon his head.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja lupasi lupauksen, sanoen: Herra Zebaot! jos sinä armossa katsoisit piikas ahdistusta, muistaisit minua ja et unhottaisi piikaas, mutta antaisit piialles pojan; niin minä annan hänen Herralle kaikiksi hänen ikäpäiviksensä, ja partaveitsi ei pidä hänen päähänsä tuleman.
verses.king_james: And she vowed a vow, and said, O Lord of hosts, if thou wilt indeed look on the affliction of thine handmaid, and remember me, and not forget thine handmaid, but wilt give unto thine handmaid a man child, then I will give him unto the Lord all the days of his life, and there shall no razor come upon his head.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem, cum illa multiplicaret preces coram Domino, ut Heli observaret os ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָה֙ כִּ֣י הִרְבְּתָ֔ה לְהִתְפַּלֵּ֖ל לִפְנֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֑ה וְעֵלִ֖י שֹׁמֵ֥ר אֶת־פִּֽיהָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And it came to pass, as she multiplied prayers before the Lord, that Heli observed her mouth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin hän kauvan rukoili Herran edessä, otti Eli vaarin hänen suustansa.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, as she continued praying before the Lord, that Eli marked her mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro Anna loquebatur in corde suo, tantumque labia illius movebantur, et vox penitus non audiebatur. Æstimavit ergo eam Heli temulentam,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְחַנָּ֗ה הִ֚יא מְדַבֶּ֣רֶת עַל־לִבָּ֔הּ רַ֚ק שְׂפָתֶ֣יהָ נָּע֔וֹת וְקוֹלָ֖הּ לֹ֣א יִשָּׁמֵ֑עַ וַיַּחְשְׁבֶ֥הָ עֵלִ֖י לְשִׁכֹּרָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now Anna spoke in her heart, and only her lips moved, but her voice was not heard at all. Heli therefore thought her to be drunk,
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä Hanna puhui sydämessänsä, ja ainoastaan hänen huulensa liikkuivat, mutta hänen äänensä ei kuulunut; niin Eli luuli hänen juovuksissa olevan.
verses.king_james: Now Hannah, she spake in her heart; only her lips moved, but her voice was not heard: therefore Eli thought she had been drunken.

verses.latin_vulgate: dixitque ei : Usquequo ebria eris ? digere paulisper vinum, quo mades.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר אֵלֶ֙יהָ֙ עֵלִ֔י עַד־מָתַ֖י תִּשְׁתַּכָּרִ֑ין הָסִ֥ירִי אֶת־יֵינֵ֖ךְ מֵעָלָֽיִךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And said to her: How long wilt thou be drunk? digest a little the wine, of which thou hast taken too much.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Eli sanoi hänelle: kuinka kauvan sinä olet juovuksissa? anna viinan olla sinustas pois.
verses.king_james: And Eli said unto her, How long wilt thou be drunken? put away thy wine from thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens Anna : Nequaquam, inquit, domine mi : nam mulier infelix nimis ego sum : vinumque et omne quod inebriare potest, non bibi, sed effudi animam meam in conspectu Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּ֨עַן חַנָּ֤ה וַתֹּ֙אמֶר֙ לֹ֣א אֲדֹנִ֔י אִשָּׁ֤ה קְשַׁת־ר֙וּחַ֙ אָנֹ֔כִי וְיַ֥יִן וְשֵׁכָ֖ר לֹ֣א שָׁתִ֑יתִי וָאֶשְׁפֹּ֥ךְ אֶת־נַפְשִׁ֖י לִפְנֵ֥י יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Anna answering, said: Not so, my lord: for I am an exceeding unhappy woman, and have drunk neither wine nor any strong drink, but I have poured out my soul before the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Hanna vastasi ja sanoi: ei, herrani! minä olen murheellinen vaimo: viinaa ja väkevää juomaa en ole minä juonut, vaan olen vuodattanut sydämeni Herran edessä.
verses.king_james: And Hannah answered and said, No, my lord, I am a woman of a sorrowful spirit: I have drunk neither wine nor strong drink, but have poured out my soul before the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne reputes ancillam tuam quasi unam de filiabus Belial : quia ex multitudine doloris et mœroris mei locuta sum usque in præsens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תִּתֵּן֙ אֶת־אֲמָ֣תְךָ֔ לִפְנֵ֖י בַּת־בְּלִיָּ֑עַל כִּֽי־מֵרֹ֥ב שִׂיחִ֛י וְכַעְסִ֖י דִּבַּ֥רְתִּי עַד־הֵֽנָּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Count not thy handmaid for one of the daughters of Belial: for out of the abundance of my sorrow and grief have I spoken till now.
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä pidä piikaas irstaisena vaimona, sillä minä olen suuresta murheestani ja vaivastani tähänasti puhunut.
verses.king_james: Count not thine handmaid for a daughter of Belial: for out of the abundance of my complaint and grief have I spoken hitherto.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc Heli ait ei : Vade in pace : et Deus Israël det tibi petitionem tuam quam rogasti eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֧עַן עֵלִ֛י וַיֹּ֖אמֶר לְכִ֣י לְשָׁל֑וֹם וֵאלֹהֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֗ל יִתֵּן֙ אֶת־שֵׁ֣לָתֵ֔ךְ אֲשֶׁ֥ר שָׁאַ֖לְתְּ מֵעִמּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Heli said to her: Go in peace: and the God of Israel grant thee thy petition, which thou hast asked of him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Eli vastasi ja sanoi: mene rauhaan, ja Israelin Jumala antakoon sinulle rukoukses, jonka sinä olet häneltä rukoillut!
verses.king_james: Then Eli answered and said, Go in peace: and the God of Israel grant thee thy petition that thou hast asked of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et illa dixit : Utinam inveniat ancilla tua gratiam in oculis tuis. Et abiit mulier in viam suam, et comedit, vultusque illius non sunt amplius in diversa mutati.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֕אמֶר תִּמְצָ֧א שִׁפְחָתְךָ֛ חֵ֖ן בְּעֵינֶ֑יךָ וַתֵּ֨לֶךְ הָאִשָּׁ֤ה לְדַרְכָּהּ֙ וַתֹּאכַ֔ל וּפָנֶ֥יהָ לֹא־הָֽיוּ־לָ֖הּ עֽוֹד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she said: Would to God thy handmaid may find grace in thy eyes. So the woman went on her way, and ate, and her countenance was no more changed.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi: anna sinun piikas löytää armo silmäis edessä. Niin vaimo meni matkaansa ja söi, ja ei ollut silleen niin murheellinen.
verses.king_james: And she said, Let thine handmaid find grace in thy sight. So the woman went her way, and did eat, and her countenance was no more sad.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et surrexerunt mane, et adoraverunt coram Domino : reversique sunt, et venerunt in domum suam Ramatha.   Cognovit autem Elcana Annam uxorem suam : et recordatus est ejus Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּשְׁכִּ֣מוּ בַבֹּ֗קֶר וַיִּֽשְׁתַּחֲווּ֙ לִפְנֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֔ה וַיָּשֻׁ֛בוּ וַיָּבֹ֥אוּ אֶל־בֵּיתָ֖ם הָרָמָ֑תָה וַיֵּ֤דַע אֶלְקָנָה֙ אֶת־חַנָּ֣ה אִשְׁתּ֔וֹ וַֽיִּזְכְּרֶ֖הָ יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they rose in the morning, and worshipped before the Lord: and they returned, and came into their house at Ramatha. And Elcana knew Anna his wife: And the Lord remembered her.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he nousivat varhain huomeneltain rukoilemaan Herraa; sitte palasivat he ja tulivat kotiansa Ramaan. Ja Elkana tunsi emäntänsä Hannan, ja Herra muisti häntä.
verses.king_james: And they rose up in the morning early, and worshipped before the Lord, and returned, and came to their house to Ramah: and Elkanah knew Hannah his wife; and the Lord remembered her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est post circulum dierum, concepit Anna, et peperit filium : vocavitque nomen ejus Samuel, eo quod a Domino postulasset eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְהִי֙ לִתְקֻפ֣וֹת הַיָּמִ֔ים וַתַּ֥הַר חַנָּ֖ה וַתֵּ֣לֶד בֵּ֑ן וַתִּקְרָ֤א אֶת־שְׁמוֹ֙ שְׁמוּאֵ֔ל כִּ֥י מֵיְהֹוָ֖ה שְׁאִלְתִּֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And it came to pass when the time was come about, Anna conceived and bore a son, and called his name Samuel: because she had asked him of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin muutamat päivät olivat kuluneet, tuli Hanna raskaaksi ja synnytti pojan ja kutsui hänen nimensä Samuel: sillä minä olen häntä rukoillut Herralta.
verses.king_james: Wherefore it came to pass, when the time was come about after Hannah had conceived, that she bare a son, and called his name Samuel, saying, Because I have asked him of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ascendit autem vir ejus Elcana, et omnis domus ejus, ut immolaret Domino hostiam solemnem, et votum suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֛עַל הָאִ֥ישׁ אֶלְקָנָ֖ה וְכׇל־בֵּית֑וֹ לִזְבֹּ֧חַ לַיהֹוָ֛ה אֶת־זֶ֥בַח הַיָּמִ֖ים וְאֶת־נִדְרֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Elcana, her husband, went up, and all his house, to offer to the Lord the solemn sacrifice, and his vow.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Elkana meni ylös kaiken huoneensa kanssa, uhraamaan Herralle vuosikautista uhria ja lupaustansa.
verses.king_james: And the man Elkanah, and all his house, went up to offer unto the Lord the yearly sacrifice, and his vow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Anna non ascendit : dixit enim viro suo : Non vadam donec ablactetur infans, et ducam eum, ut appareat ante conspectum Domini, et maneat ibi jugiter.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְחַנָּ֖ה לֹ֣א עָלָ֑תָה כִּֽי־אָמְרָ֣ה לְאִישָׁ֗הּ עַ֣ד יִגָּמֵ֤ל הַנַּ֙עַר֙ וַהֲבִאֹתִ֗יו וְנִרְאָה֙ אֶת־פְּנֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֔ה וְיָ֥שַׁב שָׁ֖ם עַד־עוֹלָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Anna went not up: for she said to her husband: I will not go till the child be weaned, and till I may carry him, that he may appear before the Lord, and may abide always there.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ei Hanna mennyt ylös, vaan sanoi miehellensä: kuin lapsi vieroitetaan, niin minä otan hänen kanssani näyttääkseni Herralle, ja sitte pitää hän siellä aina oleman.
verses.king_james: But Hannah went not up; for she said unto her husband, I will not go up until the child be weaned, and then I will bring him, that he may appear before the Lord, and there abide for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait ei Elcana vir suus : Fac quod bonum tibi videtur, et mane donec ablactes eum : precorque ut impleat Dominus verbum suum. Mansit ergo mulier, et lactavit filium suum, donec amoveret eum a lacte.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר לָהּ֩ אֶלְקָנָ֨ה אִישָׁ֜הּ עֲשִׂ֧י הַטּ֣וֹב בְּעֵינַ֗יִךְ שְׁבִי֙ עַד־גׇּמְלֵ֣ךְ אֹת֔וֹ אַ֛ךְ יָקֵ֥ם יְהֹוָ֖ה אֶת־דְּבָר֑וֹ וַתֵּ֤שֶׁב הָֽאִשָּׁה֙ וַתֵּ֣ינֶק אֶת־בְּנָ֔הּ עַד־גׇּמְלָ֖הּ אֹתֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Elcana, her husband, said to her: Do what seemeth good to thee, and stay till thou wean him: and I pray that the Lord may fulfil his word. So the woman staid at home, and gave her son suck, till she weaned him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Elkana hänen miehensä sanoi hänelle: tee niinkuin sinulle parhaaksi näkyy, ole niinkauvan ettäs hänen vieroitat: Herra vahvistakoon sen minkä hän puhunut on. Niin vaimo jäi kotia ja imetti poikaansa, siihenasti että hän vieroitti hänen.
verses.king_james: And Elkanah her husband said unto her, Do what seemeth thee good; tarry until thou have weaned him; only the Lord establish his word. So the woman abode, and gave her son suck until she weaned him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et adduxit eum secum, postquam ablactaverat, in vitulis tribus, et tribus modiis farinæ, et amphora vini, et adduxit eum ad domum Domini in Silo. Puer autem erat adhuc infantulus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּעֲלֵ֨הוּ עִמָּ֜הּ כַּאֲשֶׁ֣ר גְּמָלַ֗תּוּ בְּפָרִ֤ים שְׁלֹשָׁה֙ וְאֵיפָ֨ה אַחַ֥ת קֶ֙מַח֙ וְנֵ֣בֶל יַ֔יִן וַתְּבִאֵ֥הוּ בֵית־יְהֹוָ֖ה שִׁל֑וֹ וְהַנַּ֖עַר נָֽעַר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And after she had weaned him, she carried him with her, with three calves, and three bushels of flour, and a bottle of wine, and she brought him to the house of the Lord in Silo. Now the child was as yet very young:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän toi hänen ylös kanssansa, sittekuin hän hänen vieroittanut oli, kolmen mullin kanssa, yhden ephan jauhoja ja leilin viinaa: ja toi hänen Herran huoneesen Silossa, ja nuorukainen oli vielä sangen nuori.
verses.king_james: And when she had weaned him, she took him up with her, with three bullocks, and one ephah of flour, and a bottle of wine, and brought him unto the house of the Lord in Shiloh: and the child was young.

verses.latin_vulgate: et immolaverunt vitulum, et obtulerunt puerum Heli.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיִּשְׁחֲט֖וּ אֶת־הַפָּ֑ר וַיָּבִ֥אוּ אֶת־הַנַּ֖עַר אֶל־עֵלִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they immolated a calf, and offered the child to Heli.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he teurastivat mullin, ja toivat nuorukaisen Elin eteen.
verses.king_james: And they slew a bullock, and brought the child to Eli.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Anna : Obsecro mi domine, vivit anima tua, domine : ego sum illa mulier, quæ steti coram te hic orans Dominum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֙אמֶר֙ בִּ֣י אֲדֹנִ֔י חֵ֥י נַפְשְׁךָ֖ אֲדֹנִ֑י אֲנִ֣י הָאִשָּׁ֗ה הַנִּצֶּ֤בֶת עִמְּכָה֙ בָּזֶ֔ה לְהִתְפַּלֵּ֖ל אֶל־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Anna said: I beseech thee, my lord, as thy soul liveth, my lord: I am that woman, who stood before thee here praying to the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi: Ah, herrani! niin totta kuin sinun sielus elää, herrani, minä olen se vaimo, joka tässä tykönäs seisoi ja rukoili Herraa.
verses.king_james: And she said, Oh my lord, as thy soul liveth, my lord, I am the woman that stood by thee here, praying unto the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pro puero isto oravi, et dedit mihi Dominus petitionem meam quam postulavi eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶל־הַנַּ֥עַר הַזֶּ֖ה הִתְפַּלָּ֑לְתִּי וַיִּתֵּ֨ן יְהֹוָ֥ה לִי֙ אֶת־שְׁאֵ֣לָתִ֔י אֲשֶׁ֥ר שָׁאַ֖לְתִּי מֵעִמּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: For this child did I pray, and the Lord hath granted me my petition, which I asked of him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tätä nuorukaista minä anoin: nyt on Herra minulle antanut minun rukoukseni, jonka minä häneltä rukoilin.
verses.king_james: For this child I prayed; and the Lord hath given me my petition which I asked of him:

verses.latin_vulgate: Idcirco et ego commodavi eum Domino cunctis diebus quibus fuerit commodatus Domino. Et adoraverunt ibi Dominum.   Et oravit Anna, et ait :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְגַ֣ם אָנֹכִ֗י הִשְׁאִלְתִּ֙הוּ֙ לַֽיהֹוָ֔ה כׇּל־הַיָּמִים֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר הָיָ֔ה ה֥וּא שָׁא֖וּל לַיהֹוָ֑ה וַיִּשְׁתַּ֥חוּ שָׁ֖ם לַֽיהֹוָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Therefore I also have lent him to the Lord all the days of his life, he shall be lent to the Lord. And they adored the Lord there. And Anna prayed, and said:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sentähden annan minä hänen Herralle jälleen kaikeksi elinajaksensa, että hän Herralta rukoiltu on; ja hän rukoili Herraa siinä paikassa.
verses.king_james: Therefore also I have lent him to the Lord; as long as he liveth he shall be lent to the Lord. And he worshipped the Lord there.
